task_completion.description: Select the pre-loaded 'TumorModel_Rough' surface model for processing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'TumorModel_Rough' model is visible in the 3D viewer or selected in a module's input node selector (e.g., Surface Toolbox or Dynamic Modeler).

task_completion.description: Apply a smoothing operation to remove stair-step artifacts.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A smoothing filter is applied (e.g., Taubin or Laplacian smoothing in Surface Toolbox), and the 3D viewer shows a noticeably smoother surface compared to the initial jagged, stair-step appearance.

task_completion.description: Preserve the overall shape and volume of the original tumor model.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The smoothed model in the 3D viewer occupies roughly the same bounding box and volume as the original model, without excessive shrinking, collapsing, or distortion.
The Data module, Models module, or output node selector shows the new or modified model explicitly named 'TumorModel_Smoothed'.

task_completion.description: Save the smoothed model to the specified directory as a .vtk file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Save Data' dialog is open, showing the file path set to '~/Documents/SlicerData/BraTS/' and the filename set to 'TumorModel_Smoothed.vtk' with the save checkbox selected.

integrity.description: The agent used the application's graphical user interface to perform the smoothing and saving operations.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with GUI elements like module panels, sliders, and save dialogs, rather than just executing a Python script or terminal command to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The agent actually applied a smoothing filter rather than just renaming the original file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D viewer displays a distinct visual difference between the rough and smoothed states, confirming a filter was processed.

integrity.description: The agent performed the task inside the appropriate medical imaging software (e.g., 3D Slicer).
integrity.visual_evidence: The main application window is visible and actively used, not just a file explorer or text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses the BraTS 2021 dataset (case BraTS2021_00000). The evaluation requires a minimum roughness reduction of 30%, a maximum volume change of 10%, a maximum bounds change of 5%, and a minimum polygon count of 1000. While exact percentages are difficult to verify visually, ensure the smoothed model is noticeably smoother (stair-steps removed) but has not shrunk significantly or lost its core shape.
